museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for December 2021. Recent enough to need careful human review before verification.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, with month-specific anchors first where possible.

items.title: Halo Infinite
items.body: Master Chief returns into a semi-open ring and a live-service multiplayer future.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2021 calendar lists December 8.

items.title: Hitman 3
items.body: IO Interactive closes the World of Assassination trilogy with elegant murder-box design.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2021 calendar lists January 20.

items.title: Super Mario 3D World + Bowser's Fury
items.body: Nintendo turns a Wii U rescue mission into a Switch-era co-op object.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2021 calendar lists February 12.

items.title: Bravely Default II
items.body: Square Enix keeps old-school job systems alive on a modern handheld.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2021 calendar lists February 26.

items.title: It Takes Two
items.body: Hazelight makes co-op mandatory, theatrical and oddly intimate.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2021 calendar lists March 26.

items.title: Monster Hunter Rise
items.body: Capcom makes hunting feel nimble, portable and lockdown-social.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2021 calendar lists March 26.

items.title: Returnal
items.body: Housemarque gives PS5 a punishing loop of alien rain and haptic dread.
items.sourceNote: Returnal sources list April 30, 2021.

items.title: Resident Evil Village
items.body: Capcom turns horror tourism into one of the year's loudest shared images.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2021 calendar lists May 7.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PS5 and Xbox Series stock scarcity defines the year
items.body: The first full new-generation year is also a year of queues, stock alerts and disappointment.
items.sourceNote: Axios and Ars Technica document demand and semiconductor shortage context.

items.title: Nintendo Switch OLED is announced and launched
items.body: Nintendo refreshes the Switch with a better screen rather than a power jump.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot/Gematsu list July announcement and October 8 launch.

items.title: Microsoft completes the Bethesda/ZeniMax acquisition
items.body: Xbox's platform identity shifts toward Game Pass and first-party library scale.
items.sourceNote: Xbox Wire confirms March 9, 2021 completion.

items.title: Steam Deck is announced, then delayed
items.body: Valve's handheld PC future is visible in 2021 but slips into 2022.
items.sourceNote: Ars Technica and Steam Deck sources note the delay to February 2022.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How players discovered, argued about and shared the month.

items.title: Stock alerts become a ritual
items.body: For UK players chasing PS5 or Series X, Discord pings and retailer trackers can feel like part of the console.

items.title: Lockdown habits linger
items.body: Online co-op, Game Pass queues and backlog talk still carry the texture of pandemic-era play.

items.title: Live-service expectation keeps growing
items.body: Halo Infinite's multiplayer, seasonal structures and progression arguments show the new normal.

items.title: Digital showcases replace crowds
items.body: Directs, State of Plays and online events keep acting as the museum entrance hall.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A short atmospheric reading from the player side of recent history.

items.title: The new generation existed but was hard to touch
items.body: PS5 and Series X felt real in screenshots and friends' photos, less so in shops.

items.title: The home was still the arcade
items.body: Co-op, loops and online seasons suited a world still half inside.

items.title: Nintendo made patience feel rewarded
items.body: Metroid Dread and Switch OLED gave October a rare old/new harmony.

items.title: The future came through subscriptions
items.body: Game Pass made buying feel less like ownership and more like access.
